stories.headline: MCP crosses 97 million monthly SDK downloads — from 2M at launch to foundational agentic infrastructure
stories.summary: The Model Context Protocol reached 97 million monthly SDK downloads in March 2026 — up from approximately 2 million at its November 2024 launch — with 5,800+ community and enterprise servers now covering databases, CRMs, cloud providers, and developer tools. The milestone signals MCP has crossed from 'interesting experiment' to required knowledge for AI agent builders, with every major AI company adopting it as the standard protocol for connecting agents to external systems.
stories.lateral_explanation: When railways proliferated in the 19th century, incompatible gauge widths between competing companies made freight transfers slow and expensive — until the Gauge Act of 1846 forced standardization across Britain. MCP is playing the same role in AI agent infrastructure: multiple competing protocol...

stories.headline: Codex broad release: first-class plugins, readable multi-agent paths, and new app-server TUI capabilities
stories.summary: OpenAI shipped a broad Codex release with plugins as a first-class workflow (auto-sync at startup, browse via /plugins), sub-agents now addressable via readable path addresses like /root/agent_a with structured inter-agent messaging, and new TUI capabilities including prompt recall, shell commands, and remote websocket connections. The multi-agent v2 architecture makes agent-to-agent communication explicit and auditable — a direct response to enterprise security concerns about untracked agent orchestration.
stories.lateral_explanation: Air traffic controllers don't pilot planes — they manage separation, sequencing, and handoffs between aircraft using structured communication protocols with unique identifiers for every aircraft. Codex's readable path-based agent addresses and structured inter-agent messaging implement the same f...

stories.headline: ChatGPT expands shopping with Agentic Commerce Protocol + Walmart in-app experience
stories.summary: OpenAI expanded shopping in ChatGPT with richer product discovery, side-by-side comparisons, and up-to-date results powered by the Agentic Commerce Protocol, alongside a native Walmart in-ChatGPT app available to free, Go, Plus, and Pro users. The Walmart integration marks ChatGPT's first retail partnership with a named brick-and-mortar retailer, positioning the assistant as a purchase agent rather than just a product search tool.
stories.lateral_explanation: The Sears Roebuck catalog of the 1890s turned buying from a local transaction requiring physical presence into a text-mediated one: you described what you wanted, and a fulfillment system matched it. ChatGPT shopping reverses this exact structure — you describe what you want in conversation, and ...

stories.headline: Claude Apps ships interactive charts and visualizations directly in Claude mobile chat
stories.summary: Anthropic released Claude Apps — a feature that enables fully interactive apps with live charts, diagrams, and shareable visual assets rendered directly inside Claude conversations on iOS and Android, without switching to a separate tool or browser. The capability closes the gap between Claude's analytical reasoning and visual output, allowing users to explore data interactively in the same conversation where they asked the question.
stories.lateral_explanation: Portable sundials from the 15th century collapsed the observatory into a pocket object: complex astronomical calculation became accessible to anyone at any location without specialized infrastructure. Claude Apps follows the same logic of instrument miniaturization — collapsing the data analysis ...

stories.headline: Microsoft M365 E7 + Agent 365 governance platform: GA May 1 at $15/user as enterprise agent control plane
stories.summary: Microsoft announced M365 E7 — a new enterprise suite combining M365 E5, Copilot, Entra Suite, and the new Agent 365 governance platform — with Agent 365 giving IT and security teams a single place to observe, secure, and govern every AI agent in the organization at $15/user/month from May 1. The product treats AI agent governance as a standalone, billable control function separate from both the agents and the identity stack.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to constitutional separation of powers
stories.lateral_explanation: The separation of powers in constitutional government was an architectural solution to a specific problem: concentrating executive, legislative, and judicial power in a single actor creates unacceptable risk regardless of that actor's capability. Agent 365 applies identical logic to AI agents: se...

stories.headline: OpenClaw surpasses React as GitHub's most-starred software project at 325K+ stars
stories.summary: OpenClaw — Austrian developer Peter Steinberger's open-source autonomous AI agent that runs locally across any OS and messaging platform — surpassed 325,000 GitHub stars this month, exceeding React, Vue, and every other software project in GitHub's history after going from 9,000 to over 250,000 stars in 60 days. The founder joined OpenAI in February and moved the project to an open-source foundation, making OpenClaw the community's canonical reference for local autonomous agent architecture.
stories.lateral_explanation: Pre-internet fanzines proliferated not because they were commercially optimal but because photocopiers and staples lowered the production barrier to near zero, enabling any fan community to self-publish. OpenClaw's local-first, self-hosted model does the same for autonomous agents: by eliminating...

stories.headline: Hindsight by Vectorize: open-source agent memory hits 91% on LongMemEval, trending on GitHub
stories.summary: Vectorize AI released Hindsight — an open-source agent memory system modeled on human long-term memory, extracting structured facts, resolving entities, and building a knowledge graph organized into episodic, semantic, and procedural memory types — reaching 91% on LongMemEval and climbing to GitHub's top 20 trending in mid-March 2026. The system integrates with the MCP ecosystem as a memory server and can upgrade existing agents without redesigning their architecture.
stories.lateral_explanation: The method of loci, described by Cicero and used by Roman orators, structured memory retrieval by linking information to physical locations in a mentally constructed space — enabling recall of long speeches through spatial navigation rather than brute memorization. Hindsight builds the same archi...

stories.headline: Claude Sonnet 4.6 ships with 1M token context window in beta — Anthropic's fastest Sonnet upgrade
stories.summary: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 4.6 as a full upgrade across coding, computer use, long-context reasoning, agent planning, and knowledge work, with a 1 million token context window now in beta — the largest context available in any Sonnet-tier model from any major lab. The model is now the default powering Cowork and Claude Code's autonomous modes.
stories.lateral_explanation: Bats don't see the world in snapshots — they perceive continuous spatial maps built from reflected sound, giving them simultaneous situational awareness of their entire environment. A 1 million token context window moves language models toward an analogous continuous awareness: rather than readin...

stories.headline: MCP 2026 Roadmap published: batch operations, resource subscriptions, and native auth incoming
stories.summary: The Model Context Protocol team published its 2026 roadmap, announcing three structural upgrades: batch operations for multi-tool calls in a single round-trip, resource subscriptions for real-time context updates, and a native authentication layer replacing per-server OAuth workarounds. The roadmap directly addresses the three most commonly cited production pain points limiting MCP adoption in large enterprise deployments.
stories.lateral_explanation: IPv6 was designed in 1998 to solve IPv4's address exhaustion, but most of the internet only migrated after 2010 when the practical pain of address scarcity became acute enough to force action. MCP's 2026 roadmap upgrades follow the same pattern: the protocol launched to solve the immediate connec...

stories.headline: Copilot Cowork: Microsoft builds cloud AI agent across all M365 apps with Anthropic's help
stories.summary: Microsoft announced Copilot Cowork — a cloud-based AI agentic automation tool integrated into Microsoft 365 Copilot that can complete work on users' behalf across Word, Excel, Outlook, Teams, and other M365 apps, built in collaboration with Anthropic. Broader availability is coming through Microsoft's Frontier program in late March 2026, positioning M365 as a platform where AI agents execute cross-app workflows rather than assisting within individual apps.
stories.lateral_explanation: In mid-20th century corporate offices, secretarial pools provided centralized support services — typing, scheduling, filing — accessible across departments without each requiring dedicated staff. Copilot Cowork is an architectural return to pooled support: a centralized AI agent capable of taking...

stories.headline: Microsoft Agent 365: enterprise governance control plane for AI agents, GA May 1 at $15/user/month
stories.summary: Microsoft's Agent 365 will be generally available May 1 at $15/user/month as the first purpose-built enterprise product for AI agent governance: IT and security teams get a single control plane to observe, secure, and govern every agent running across the organization. The product treats AI agent management as a distinct enterprise function requiring dedicated tooling, separate from both the agents themselves and the identity and access management stack they depend on.
stories.lateral_explanation: The Nuremberg Code (1947) established that experimentation on humans requires informed consent and oversight — not because researchers were malicious, but because power asymmetries between experimenter and subject required formal protection structures. Agent 365 applies the same logic to AI agent...

stories.headline: 78% of enterprises run AI agent pilots — but only 14% have scaled to organization-wide use
stories.summary: A March 2026 survey of 650 enterprise technology leaders found that 78% are running at least one AI agent pilot, but only 14% have successfully scaled an agent to organization-wide operational use — a 5.6:1 gap persisting despite two years of high investment and strong vendor support. Organizations with mature governance frameworks report the highest production deployment rates, identifying policy, access control, and audit trails as more limiting than model capability.
stories.lateral_explanation: Geoffrey Moore's 'Crossing the Chasm' identified a consistent gap between early adopter enthusiasm and mainstream market penetration — the chasm being the period when a technology works for enthusiasts but hasn't proven itself in pragmatic organizational contexts. The 78%/14% pilot-to-production ...

stories.headline: Gartner: 40%+ of agentic AI projects will be cancelled by end of 2027 — cost and unclear value the primary drivers
stories.summary: Gartner's March 2026 forecast predicts more than 40% of currently active agentic AI projects will be cancelled by end of 2027, citing escalating infrastructure costs, inability to demonstrate measurable business value, and inadequate risk controls as primary causes. The prediction is a structural correction to deployment optimism of 2025 — enterprise project discipline catching up to experimental enthusiasm.
stories.lateral_explanation: Natural selection doesn't eliminate species — it eliminates organisms poorly adapted to environmental conditions, strengthening the fitness of the surviving population. Gartner's forecast of 40%+ project cancellations is the equivalent: poorly adapted AI projects (unclear scope, no measurable val...

stories.headline: Dataiku: the 2026 manufacturing AI mandate is moving from pilot to profit, not just pilot to production
stories.summary: Dataiku's March 2026 manufacturing analysis reframes the enterprise AI challenge: the transition point is not 'pilot to production' but 'pilot to profit,' as companies running 15+ production agent pilots still see diminishing returns without a measurable financial outcome model. Predictive maintenance loops, quality control agents, and supply chain coordination emerge as the use cases with the highest ratio of measurable financial return to deployment complexity.
stories.lateral_explanation: Toyota's quality circles in the 1970s shifted manufacturing improvement from top-down engineering decisions to localized, continuous feedback loops at the production level — the insight being that improvement compounds when embedded in the work rather than managed above it. Dataiku's 2026 framewo...

stories.headline: 84% of tech leaders plan to expand their workforce as a result of AI — the displacement narrative shifts
stories.summary: A March 2026 enterprise technology survey found that 84% of tech leaders anticipate expanding their workforce in the next six months as a direct result of AI implementation, citing new roles in agent management, AI governance, and human-AI workflow design as primary drivers. The finding challenges the dominant AI-reduces-headcount narrative by capturing net workforce effects at organizations where AI is already running in production.
stories.lateral_explanation: The Luddite movement of the 1810s destroyed textile machinery not because machines eliminated all work, but because they redistributed the value of skilled craft to machine operators and factory owners, destroying the economic position of skilled weavers. The 84% workforce expansion data suggests...

stories.headline: ServiceNow positions as 'AI Control Tower' for IT and HR workflows alongside Salesforce
stories.summary: ServiceNow is running an enterprise AI strategy parallel to Salesforce Agentforce's CRM focus — targeting IT ticketing, HR case management, and cross-system workflow orchestration as its defensible domains. Large enterprises are deploying both systems simultaneously, with ServiceNow handling operational workflows and Salesforce covering customer-facing revenue processes — a two-platform pattern that may define enterprise AI architecture for the next 5 years.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to air traffic control tower design
stories.lateral_explanation: Air traffic control towers are architecturally designed to provide maximum visibility over all aircraft movements while maintaining physical separation from the runways themselves — oversight without direct operational involvement in each flight. ServiceNow's 'AI Control Tower' positioning descri...

stories.headline: Salesforce Q4 FY26: $11.18B revenue (+11.7% YoY), Agentforce AI driving deal sizes 3x larger than non-AI
stories.summary: Salesforce reported Q4 FY26 revenue of $11.18 billion — an 11.7% year-over-year increase — with Agentforce explicitly cited as the driver of deals averaging 3x the contract value of equivalent non-AI engagements. The results confirm Salesforce as the primary 'buy, don't build' beneficiary in the enterprise AI agent market: customers are purchasing integrated Agentforce ecosystems rather than building custom solutions.
stories.lateral_explanation: Platform economics explains why marketplaces systematically produce larger transactions than point solutions: buyers purchasing from a platform are buying into an ecosystem of interconnected services, not just a single feature. Salesforce's 3x deal size for Agentforce-enabled contracts is platfor...

stories.headline: Customer service AI agents save teams 40+ hours/month and accelerate finance close cycles by 30-50%
stories.summary: New 2026 enterprise data shows AI agents handling customer service (refunds, escalations, omnichannel support) are saving teams 40+ hours monthly, while finance agents automating invoicing and expense auditing are accelerating close processes by 30-50% — the most concrete and frequently cited operational ROI metrics from companies with agents running in production. Results align with G2's median findings of 40% cost reduction per unit and 80% customer service incident containment.
stories.lateral_explanation: Frederick Winslow Taylor spent the 1890s measuring the precise time required for discrete physical tasks — shoveling, assembly, inspection — to optimize manufacturing by eliminating motion that consumed time without adding value. The '40+ hours/month saved' metric in customer service AI deploymen...

stories.headline: Global software spending reaches $1.43 trillion in 2026 — AI-related spend projected to triple over 2025-26
stories.summary: Gartner projects global software spending will grow 14.7% to $1.43 trillion in 2026, with AI-related spending expected to triple over the two-year 2025-2026 period — the largest two-year software investment cycle in recorded industry history. The acceleration is driven primarily by enterprises replacing point-solution automation tools with AI agent platforms that handle multiple workflows simultaneously.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to historical infrastructure investment cycles
stories.lateral_explanation: Historical infrastructure investment cycles — canals (1790s), railways (1840s), electricity (1890s), internet (1990s) — all followed the same S-curve: explosive investment in infrastructure, followed by consolidation as the infrastructure becomes a foundation layer rather than the growth story it...

stories.headline: LangChain State of Agent Engineering 2026: dominant enterprise pattern is human-in-the-loop with isolated, narrow agent tasks
stories.summary: LangChain's State of Agent Engineering 2026 survey found that the dominant production pattern is not fully autonomous multi-agent pipelines but isolated, single-purpose agents with human approval gates at consequential decision points — directly contradicting the 'autonomous AI employee' narrative. The most successful deployments share three characteristics: narrow task scope, explicit failure modes, and a designated human review step before any irreversible action.
stories.lateral_explanation: Nassim Taleb's barbell strategy places assets at both extremes — very safe and very speculative — avoiding the 'mediocre middle' that appears safe but is exposed to tail risk. LangChain's finding that successful enterprise agents are either very narrow (low risk, tightly scoped) or fully autonomo...

stories.headline: Viernes de Dolores: las primeras procesiones de Semana Santa 2026 salen esta noche por Madrid
stories.summary: Esta noche arrancan dos cofradías que marcan el inicio oficial de la Semana Santa madrileña 2026: el Santísimo Cristo del Perdón por Puente de Vallecas (19:30, nazarenos en granate con capirote de 90 cm) y el Cristo del Pozo y Nuestra Señora de Dolores (20:00, con el encuentro entre el Cristo y su madre al final del recorrido). El Viernes de Dolores inicia nueve días de procesiones distribuidas por todos los distritos de la capital hasta el 5 de abril.
stories.lateral_explanation: Medieval mystery plays used the entire city as a stage — the audience moved through streets that became sets, and the procession route was the dramaturgy. Semana Santa procesiones are a direct survival of this urban theater tradition: the pasos are props, the hermandades are the cast, and Valleca...

stories.headline: Yurena: 'El concierto que nunca fue' esta noche en Madrid — 2.000 entradas, 12 bailarines, show inédito
stories.summary: Yurena actúa esta noche con 'El concierto que nunca fue' — un espectáculo declarado único e irrepetible con más de 2.000 entradas vendidas, 12 bailarines en escena, nuevas versiones de sus canciones más icónicas y múltiples invitados sorpresa. El concierto materializa uno de los eventos más esperados del pop español: una artista que nunca había dado un show de esta escala finalmente lo hace.
stories.lateral_explanation: When an artist who never performed live stages their first major concert, the event functions as cultural archaeology: audiences aren't just attending a show, they're participating in the reconstruction of a cultural artifact that existed only incompletely. Yurena's 'El concierto que nunca fue' m...

stories.headline: Tulipanes en flor: Plaza de Oriente gratis, Real Jardín Botánico esta semana
stories.summary: El jardín de la Plaza de Oriente, frente al Teatro Real, es esta semana el punto de floración de tulipanes más accesible de Madrid — entrada completamente gratuita en pleno centro histórico con el Palacio Real de fondo. El Real Jardín Botánico ofrece una experiencia más completa con mayor variedad de especies por unos 6 euros de entrada, en un espacio que lleva registrando la floración de primavera desde 1781.
stories.lateral_explanation: The Dutch Tulip Mania of 1636-37 was the first recorded speculative bubble — caused by a flower. Tulips commanded contract prices equivalent to annual craftsman wages because their bloom was brief, their variety unpredictable, and their beauty reproducible only slowly. Watching tulips bloom for f...

stories.headline: Papagena en la azotea del Teatro Real: coctelería de autor para el Día Mundial del Teatro esta tarde
stories.summary: Papagena — el restaurante-bar en la azotea del Teatro Real — celebra hoy el Día Mundial del Teatro con una carta de cócteles de autor inspirada en las grandes obras del teatro y la ópera, con vistas directas al Palacio Real y la Plaza de Oriente. Es el único establecimiento gastronómico sobre un teatro de ópera en funcionamiento en Madrid, accesible sin entrada al espectáculo.
stories.lateral_explanation: Wagner's concept of Gesamtkunstwerk — 'total artwork' — argued that opera should synthesize music, drama, visual spectacle, and architecture into a unified aesthetic experience rather than a sequence of separate arts. Papagena on the roof of the Teatro Real collapses the boundary between gastrono...

stories.headline: 11 Nudos Rooftop en el Mercado de San Antón estrena nueva carta atlántica para compartir este fin de semana
stories.summary: La tercera planta del Mercado de San Antón estrena este fin de semana la nueva propuesta de 11 Nudos Rooftop Nordés — una cocina de acento atlántico con productos gallegos y del norte de la Península pensada para compartir en formato apertura sobre Chueca. El Mercado de San Antón, inaugurado en 2011, ha sido el espacio donde más restaurantes de autor de barrio han estrenado su primera carta grande en Madrid.
stories.lateral_explanation: The etymology of 'tapas' traces to the practice of covering (tapar) wine glasses with bread or ham to keep flies out — a functional object that became a cultural institution. Mercado de San Antón's rooftop model continues the same logic: a building primarily designed for daytime food retail devel...

books.title: Co-Intelligence: Living and Working with AI
books.author: Ethan Mollick
books.why: Mollick's Wharton research on practical human-AI collaboration directly mirrors the enterprise patterns David has been tracking this week — especially the LangChain finding that human-in-the-loop beats full autonomy. This is the most evidence-backed book on what working alongside AI actually feels like day-to-day.

books.title: AI Engineering: Building Applications with Foundation Models
books.author: Chip Huyen
books.why: With MCP hitting 97M downloads and agent infrastructure maturing fast, Huyen's systems-level framework for production AI engineering is the most practical guide to the stack David is watching — from evals to deployment patterns to agent orchestration. O'Reilly's most-read AI engineering book in 2026.
